questions.question: Write a recursive function for linear search in an array. Also derive its time and space complexity.
keyPoints: Base case: return -1 if element not found or index exceeds array bounds
Recursive case: compare current element, return index if found else recurse on next index
Space complexity: O(n) - recursive call stack

questions.question: Write a recursive binary search algorithm and explain how it differs from the iterative version in terms of space complexity.
keyPoints: Requires sorted array, compares middle element and narrows search space
Base case: element found or low > high (not found)
Recursive calls: search left half or right half based on comparison
Space complexity: O(log n) for both recursive and iterative versions

questions.question: (a) Implement recursive linear search and recursive binary search in C. (b) Compare both algorithms in terms of time complexity, space complexity, and prerequisites. (c) Under what conditions would you prefer one over the other? Justify your answer with examples.
keyPoints: Linear search: O(n) time, O(n) space, works on unsorted arrays
Binary search: O(log n) time, O(log n) space, requires sorted array
Binary search is preferred for large sorted datasets, linear search for small/unsorted data
Recursive binary search reduces search space by half each iteration

questions.question: Trace the execution of recursive binary search for element X = 15 in the sorted array [2, 5, 8, 12, 15, 18, 22, 27, 30]. Show all recursive calls with parameters (low, high) and return values. Also write the complete C code for this recursive binary search function with proper base cases and recursive cases.
keyPoints: First call: low=0, high=8, mid=4, arr[4]=15 found, return 4
Trace shows narrowing of search space: 0→4→4 (found)
Code must include: base cases (found/not found), mid calculation, recursive calls for left/right halves
Each recursive call reduces problem size by approximately half
